mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 valuations and producing negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of rising rate periods over a five-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based demand growth that sustains corporate revenue trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies in a three-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply chain constraints that trigger short-term equity sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of triggered observations or the signal shows no predictive power during documented supply shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicate persistent inflation that forces continued monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered months or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and wage growth that support corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any two-year rolling window or sixty-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of high-payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly gross domestic product contraction signals immediate recessionary fears that trigger rapid portfolio de-risking, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of contraction quarters or the signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns during historical recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside rising housing starts indicates synchronized real economy growth that boosts equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during expansionary phases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of triggered months or loses predictive accuracy when tested against periods of stagflation.
